semanticOwner: .github/workflows/claude-code-review.yml with authority and automation registers to be created by HGI-304 and HGI-306
evidence: The automatic review workflow triggers on opened and synchronized pull requests and grants read permissions plus id-token write while allowing gh pr comment.
Repository YAML provides no severity taxonomy, deduplication key, convergence condition, stopping rule, escalation route, budget, or measured false-positive/cost evidence.
GitHub readback confirms Actions are enabled and SHA pinning is not required; it does not establish review effectiveness.
rejectedAlternatives: Retain automatic review unchanged and describe it as settled or effective.
Retire automatic review without first preserving the evidence and a replacement decision.
Treat interactive @claude workflow behavior as evidence for automatic review convergence.
acceptedOrDefaultBehavior: Treat the current automatic Claude review as an ungoverned, mutation-capable experiment, not an accepted automation. HGI-304 may retain it only after establishing the actual comment credential and tightening least privilege, timeouts, concurrency, one-comment-per-PR/head-SHA deduplication, stop-after-one-run convergence, and named escalation; HGI-306 must add cost and false-positive evidence. If those controls and readbacks cannot be implemented, retire the automatic workflow in HGI-304 rather than claim safe automation.

taskBlocking.inheritedNonClaim: Do not retain automatic review as a governed capability without explicit authority and convergence controls.
Do not admit the loop to continuous automation without severity, deduplication, stopping, escalation, and cost evidence.

authorityImpact: PR comments are external GitHub mutations. This record authorizes none, does not establish which action/OIDC/token path can comment, and does not accept the current workflow as governed. Only separately authorized HGI-304 workflow changes may constrain or retire it; it must never merge, deploy, release, or approve.
nonClaims: Automatic Claude review is not accepted as non-mutating, authorized, effective, convergent, low-cost, or safe for consequential action.
Repository YAML and Actions settings do not establish the actual PR-comment credential, token issuance, permission at run time, or a successful comment.
No GitHub workflow or provider setting was changed.
carryingCost: Track run count, duplicate comments, escalation, reviewer disposition, false positives, elapsed time, and token/cost receipt per review epoch.
reviewOrResumeTrigger: Resume in HGI-304 only with actual comment-authority readback and a bounded workflow diff; retire there if least privilege, timeout, concurrency, deduplication, stopping, and escalation cannot be proved. HGI-306 may admit the retained loop only after cost and false-positive evidence.
